name: FlatBaseChange.md
content:
# Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean ‚Äî iter-012

## gammaPushforwardIso_comp (L733) ‚Äî the foundation residual

### Goal
Close `gammaPushforwardIso_comp`, whose iter-011 residual reduced to `Œì(cast) x = x`
(the `Spec.map_comp œÜ œÅ` reindexing). Directive: build 2 rw-only exposure lemmas, then close
via `eqToIso.hom`/`eqToHom_app`/`eqToHom_map` + `pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app`, NO defeq.

### Result: PARTIAL ‚Äî sorry still open, but 4 new compiling helpers + the blocker characterised precisely.

### Attempt 1 ‚Äî whole-goal defeq `exact (gammaPushforwardIso_inv_apply _ _ _).symm`
- Elaborates to `goals: []` under the LSP. **Cold build = VERIFIED KERNEL BOMB** (`(deterministic)
  timeout at whnf`). Confirms the memory: the defeq collapse forces `whnf` of the whole Spec
  structure-sheaf machinery. DEAD (do not retry).

### Attempt 2 ‚Äî planner's lemma-driven route (exposures + collapse)
Built the exposure machinery and pushed the goal to a fully-exposed form:
```
rw [gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply (œÜ ‚â´ œÅ) N x, ModuleCat.comp_apply, ModuleCat.comp_apply]
simp only [eqToIso.hom, eqToHom_app, Functor.map_comp, eqToHom_map, ModuleCat.comp_apply]
rw [moduleCat_eqToHom_concreteCategory_apply]   -- eqToHom ‚Üí SYMBOLIC cast (no whnf)
-- GOAL: x = B (A (Œ≥_œÜ (Œì_pc (cast hc x))))
```
where `B = restrictScalarsComp.inv.app`, `A = restrictScalars.map Œ≥_œÅ`, `Œ≥_œÜ = gammaPushforwardIso œÜ`,
`Œì_pc = moduleSpecŒìFunctor.map (pushforwardComp.inv.app N)`. Every wrapper is the carrier identity.

Built **5 single-layer `:= rfl` helpers (ALL COMPILE, kernel-light, axiom-free)**:
- `moduleCat_eqToHom_concreteCategory_apply` (L696) ‚Äî `ConcreteCategory.hom (eqToHom h) x = cast (‚Ä¶) x`
  (`subst h; rfl`). The kernel-light replacement for a `change`/`rfl` eqToHom collapse.
- `gammaPushforwardIso_hom_concreteApply` (L706) ‚Äî `ConcreteCategory.hom`-keyed restatement of
  `gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply`.
- `restrictScalars_map_concreteApply` (L713) ‚Äî restrictScalars preserves the underlying function.
- `restrictScalarsComp_inv_app_concreteApply` (L720) ‚Äî restrictScalarsComp.inv is carrier identity.

### THE BLOCKER (characterised precisely ‚Äî NEW information vs the iter-011/planner picture)
The planner assumed the outer wrappers `B, A, Œ≥_œÜ` "collapse by `gammaPushforwardIso_{hom,inv}_apply`"
and only the cast is genuine. **That is false.** The genuine obstruction is the
`X.Modules`/value-`ModuleCat` **diamond**, and it is BROADER than the cast:

- Every wrapper changes the **object** type ‚Äî its domain ‚âÝ codomain as `ModuleCat` objects; they
  agree ONLY on the carrier (the carrier-defeq is CHEAP: `(rfl : ‚Üë(Œì(pushforward (Spec (œÜ‚â´œÅ)) N)) =
  ‚Üë(Œì(pushforward (Spec œÅ ‚â´ Spec œÜ) N)))` typechecks instantly ‚Äî VERIFIED).
- `rw`/`simp [helper]` FAILS to fire on `Œ≥_œÜ` and `B`: rewriting `Œ≥_œÜ z ‚Üí z` retypes `z` from
  `Œ≥_œÜ`'s codomain object to its domain object, so the enclosing wrapper no longer typechecks
  WITHOUT the heavy `restrictScalars ‚Üî pushforward‚àòŒì` object identity (motive-not-type-correct).
  (`A` and the inner `Œ≥_œÅ` DO fire ‚Äî they sit under `restrictScalars.map`, a syntactic junction.)
- A term-mode `Eq.trans` chain through all five layers ELABORATES under the LSP (`goals: []`) but
  **EXPLODES on cold build** ‚Äî the junction defeqs ARE that heavy object identity at every step
  (`> 1.6M` heartbeats, VERIFIED timeout). Even the bare `simp`+`rw [moduleCat_eqToHom‚Ä¶]` SETUP for
  that chain is a cold-build (kernel) deterministic timeout.
- A single monolithic `rfl` (`rw [moduleCat_eqToHom‚Ä¶]; rfl`) is a VERIFIED cold-build KERNEL BOMB.

Essential tension: **carrier-rfl is CHEAP, per-junction OBJECT-defeq is HEAVY**, and the layered
collapse forces one heavy object-defeq per junction no matter the tactic.

### Dead ends (do NOT re-attempt)
- `exact (gammaPushforwardIso_inv_apply _ _ _).symm` ‚Äî kernel bomb (whnf timeout).
- `rw [moduleCat_eqToHom_concreteCategory_apply]; rfl` ‚Äî kernel bomb (monolithic rfl).
- term-mode `Eq.trans` chain over the 5 wrappers ‚Äî cold-build >1.6M heartbeat timeout.
- `simp only [*_concreteApply]` ‚Äî only `A`/`Œ≥_œÅ` fire; `Œ≥_œÜ`/`B` blocked by the object-junction
  diamond (motive-not-type-correct without the heavy defeq).
- `rw [Spec.map_comp œÜ œÅ]` early ‚Äî motive not type correct.
- inline `rw [show ConcreteCategory.hom (gammaPushforwardIso œÜ _).hom = ‚Ä¶ from rfl]` /
  `rw [eq_of_heq (cast_heq _ x)]` ‚Äî pattern not found (diamond instance mismatch / unresolved cast mv).

### Next step (iter-013, PRE-COMMITTED in PROGRESS.md): mathlib-analogist consult
No LLM API key in env (`archon-informal-agent` unavailable ‚Äî VERIFIED), so I could not run the
consult myself. The corrective: collapse the value-`ModuleCat` carrier-identity composite by routing
through `.val.app (op ‚ä§)` at the SHEAF level (`SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app` EXISTS
and is exactly `(((pushforwardComp œÜ œà).inv.app M).val.app U) x = x`), where the objects are the
uniform `N.val.obj ‚ä§` and the restrictScalars/globalSections object-junctions never form. The open
question for the analogist: how to express `B/A/Œ≥_œÜ` (which are restrictScalars-level, not sheaf-level)
without forming the heavy object-junction ‚Äî likely a structural lemma identifying the whole composite
`gammaPushforwardIso` object-wise, or an `Iso.ext`/inverse-comparison route that never element-chases.

## Crux `pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_natural` (L1347)
Not attempted ‚Äî the foundation `gammaPushforwardNatIso_comp` it is gated on remains transitively open
(own body proved iter-011, but it `exact`s `gammaPushforwardIso_comp` which is still `sorry`). Budget
consumed by the foundation. Left as the pre-existing typed `sorry`.

## Did NOT touch
- The 3 COMPILE-DEAD mate sorries (L1546/1821/1861) ‚Äî per directive.
- No `maxHeartbeats 1e6` added; no `set_option` cleanup.

## Needs blueprint entry
The 4 new helpers (all `lem:`-worthy, project-local, kernel-light `:= rfl`/`subst;rfl`):
- `AlgebraicGeometry.moduleCat_eqToHom_concreteCategory_apply` (L696) ‚Äî uses `eqToHom`, `cast`,
  `congrArg`. Generic `ModuleCat` eqToHom‚Üícarrier-cast.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIso_hom_concreteApply` (L706) ‚Äî uses `gammaPushforwardIso`,
  `ConcreteCategory.hom`. `ConcreteCategory.hom`-keyed twin of `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.restrictScalars_map_concreteApply` (L713) ‚Äî uses `ModuleCat.restrictScalars`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.restrictScalarsComp_inv_app_concreteApply` (L720) ‚Äî uses
  `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp`.

## Summary
- **Sorry count: 5 ‚Üí 5** (no sorries closed, no sorries added). The 5 are: `gammaPushforwardIso_comp`
  (L733, assigned ‚Äî STILL OPEN), `pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_natural` (L1347, crux), and the
  3 dead-mate sorries (L1546/1821/1861, do-not-touch).
- **Sorries closed:** none.
- **Sorries still open + why:** `gammaPushforwardIso_comp` ‚Äî blocked by the value-`ModuleCat` object-
  junction diamond (see BLOCKER); all three collapse routes (rw/simp matching, term-mode Eq.trans,
  monolithic rfl) fail with VERIFIED specific failures. Needs the iter-013 analogist consult.
- **Adjacent sorries:** did not attempt the crux (transitively gated on this foundation).
- File compiles cleanly (`lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FlatBaseChange` ‚Üí BUILD OK).

## Why I stopped
`Partial progress.` Concrete CODE advances this iter (not just prose):
1. Added 4 new helper lemmas that COMPILE and are kernel-light/axiom-free (verified by cold build) ‚Äî
   the reusable foundation any future finish needs.
2. Pushed the residual goal from iter-011's opaque `Œì(cast) x = x` to a fully-exposed
   `x = B(A(Œ≥_œÜ(Œì_pc(cast x))))` with every layer isolated and named.
3. VERIFIED (cold build) that all three plausible finishes ‚Äî whole-goal defeq, monolithic rfl, and
   the term-mode Eq.trans chain ‚Äî fail, and characterised WHY (object-junction diamond; carrier-rfl
   cheap, object-defeq heavy). This rules out the entire "collapse element-wise" family and is the
   decisive new fact for the planner.
The genuine remaining blocker is a Mathlib-idiom/structural problem (collapse a value-`ModuleCat`
carrier-identity composite without forming heavy per-junction object defeqs). No API key is present,
so the informal-agent consult must be dispatched by the planner (iter-013, already pre-committed).
This is NOT difficulty-avoidance: the element-wise route is provably exhausted; the next move is a
different (sheaf-level / structural) proof, which is a planning decision the prover lane cannot make
unilaterally without the analogist.

name: SectionGradedRing.md
content:
# Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean ‚Äî fine-grained pass (iter-012)

Objective: the associator bridge `tensorObjAssoc_eq_localizedAssociator` (was 1 sorry @L1844).
Plan: Step 0 derive a well-typed common form `K`; Step 1/2 prove the two half-assemblies
`hK_lhs`/`hK_rhs`; Step 3 assemble `= hK_lhs.trans hK_rhs.symm`.

## Sentence 0 (GATING): "the two halves meet at the common form K" ‚Üí `assocCommonForm`
- **Result: RESOLVED.** Defined `private noncomputable def assocCommonForm (A B C) : tensorObj
  (tensorObj A B) C ‚ü∂ A ‚äó_loc (B ‚äó_loc C)` (@L1847), the well-typed Lean realisation of the
  blueprint schematic `K = L'(Œ±^p) ; Œº‚Åª¬π ; (L'a ‚óÅ Œº‚Åª¬π) ; (c_A ‚äó (c_B ‚äó c_C))` **prefixed by the
  inverse segment-1 whiskered unit** `(L'(Œ∑_{a‚äób} ‚ñ∑ c))‚Åª¬π` (= the seg-1 iso of `tensorObjAssoc`).
  That prefix is exactly the "counit object-glue" the plan flagged: it fixes the domain mismatch
  between the schematic core's domain `L'((a‚äób)‚äóc)` and the assembly domain
  `tensorObj (tensorObj A B) C`.
- **K typechecks** (only needed `@asIso ‚Ä¶ (isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit ‚Ä¶)` to supply the
  seg-1 `IsIso` instance, as `tensorObjAssoc` does). The plan's gating worry ("K may not
  typecheck") is **cleared** ‚Äî surfacing per plan: K is sound.

## Sentence 3 (assembly): `tensorObjAssoc_eq_localizedAssociator`
- **Result: RESOLVED (own body sorry-free).** Now `:= (hK_lhs A B C).trans (hK_rhs A B C).symm`
  (@L1968). The `.trans/.symm` typechecks (confirms K's domain/codomain match both halves) ‚Äî the
  bridge no longer has its own sorry; it is transitively gated on the two half-lemmas.

## Sentence 1: `tensorObjAssoc_eq_localizedAssociator_hK_lhs : Œ¶^L ‚â´ Œ±^loc = K`
- **Result: PARTIAL** (sorry @L1903). Proof unfolds `assocCommonForm` + `tensorObjLocalizedIso` to
  the **isolated localized-side goal** (compiles; goal reached).
- **Precise state:** LHS `= Œº_{(A‚äóB)‚ô≠,c}.inv ‚â´ (c_{A‚äóB} ‚äó c_C) ‚â´ ((Œº_{a,b}.inv ‚â´ (c_A‚äóc_B)) ‚ñ∑ C) ‚â´
  (Œ±_ A B C).hom`; RHS `= K`. There is **no whiskered unit** on this side.
- **Blocker (named):** `Localization.Monoidal.associator_hom_app` does NOT fire on `(Œ±_ A B C).hom`
  ‚Äî verified: its LHS pattern requires the objects to be literally `(L').obj _`, but `A,B,C` are
  arbitrary. Next step (documented in-code): push the counit isos `c_A,c_B,c_C` through `Œ±_ A B C`
  via `Localization.Monoidal.associator_naturality` (+ `tensorHom_id`/`id_tensorHom`) to obtain
  `Œ±_ (L'a)(L'b)(L'c)`, THEN `associator_hom_app` makes the Œº's native and the Œº-pairs cancel.

## Sentence 2: `tensorObjAssoc_eq_localizedAssociator_hK_rhs : Œ± ‚â´ Œ¶^R = K`
- **Result: PARTIAL but substantially advanced** (sorry @L1960). The proof now executes, in order:
  1. `rw [tensorObjAssoc]` + unfold `tensorObjLocalizedIso`/`assocCommonForm` ‚Üí
     **both sides share the identical prefix** `inv(map (Œ∑_{a‚äób}‚ñ∑c)) ‚â´ map(Œ±^p) ‚â´ ‚Ä¶`.
  2. **Two `congrArg (fun t => _ ‚â´ t)`** strip that common prefix ‚Äî reduces the obligation to the
     small tail (segments 3‚Äì5 vs `Œ¶^R`). ‚úÖ works.
  3. `slice_lhs 1 3 => erw [hcol]` collapses the braiding-conjugated whiskered unit (segments 3‚Äì5)
     to `(A ‚óÅ Œ∑_{b‚äóc})^#` (reusing the proven seam
     `sheafification_braiding_whiskerRight_unit_eq_whiskerLeft_unit` after the symmetric swap). ‚úÖ.
  4. `erw [sheafification_whiskerLeft_unit_eq_mu' ‚Ä¶]` rewrites it to a Œº-conjugate, placing the
     keystone's trailing `Œº_{a,(L'(b‚äóc))‚ô≠}.hom` adjacent to the `Œº_{a,(L'(b‚äóc))‚ô≠}.inv` from
     `i_{A,B‚äóC}` (**identical object** `B.tensorObj C`). ‚úÖ.
- **Blocker (named, verified in isolation ‚Äî corrects a prior plan assumption):** the Œº-pair
  `Œº_{a,(L'(b‚äóc))‚ô≠}.hom ‚â´ Œº_{a,(L'(b‚äóc))‚ô≠}.inv` still **does NOT cancel even on this small isolated
  goal**. The keystone RHS is a grouped composite `(Œº.inv ‚â´ L'Œ∑ ‚â´ Œº.hom)` whose inner `‚â´` is the
  `Localization.Monoidal` localized-category `CategoryStruct.comp`, while the boundary `‚â´ Œº.inv`
  from `tensorObjLocalizedIso` is the `X.Modules`-synonym `comp`; defeq but NOT syntactic. So
  `simp only [Category.assoc]` makes **no progress** (cannot re-associate across the boundary), and
  `rw`/`erw [Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]`, `slice ‚Ä¶ rw [Iso.hom_inv_id]`, `rw [‚Üê Category.assoc,
  Iso.eq_comp_inv]` all fail to find the adjacent `Œº.hom ‚â´ Œº.inv`. Forcing it with
  `simp only [Localization.Monoidal.Œº]` (unfold to `tensorBifunctorIso`) **times out at isDefEq**.
- **Residual after the (blocked) cancel** = the counit coherence
  `(L'a ‚óÅ L'Œ∑_{bc}) ‚â´ (c_A ‚äó c_{bc}) ‚â´ (A ‚óÅ (Œº_{bc}.inv ‚â´ (c_B‚äóc_C))) = (L'a ‚óÅ Œº_{bc}.inv) ‚â´
  (c_A ‚äó (c_B‚äóc_C))` (`Œº_natural_right` + the sheafification unit/counit triangle `L'Œ∑ ‚â´ c = id`).

## Summary
- **Sentences:** 2/4 RESOLVED (the GATING `K` definition + the assembly own-body), 2/4 PARTIAL with
  precise blockers (`hK_lhs`, `hK_rhs`).
- **Sorry count (this file, my region):** the assembly's 1 sorry @L1844 ‚Üí assembly now sorry-free;
  +2 new sorries in the isolated half-lemmas (`hK_lhs` @L1903, `hK_rhs` @L1960). Net +1 sorry, but
  the residual is now isolated and precisely characterised (and `hK_rhs` is reduced through
  collapse + keystone all the way to the single Œº-cancel + counit coherence).
- **Cold build:** `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SectionGradedRing` ‚Üí **success, 2441 jobs**
  (only `sorry`/style warnings; no kernel timeout ‚Äî the `erw`/`slice`/`congrArg` chain is
  kernel-light). No `maxHeartbeats 1e6` added.

## Why I stopped (honest)
- **Real progress:** GATING passed (well-typed `K` exists ‚Äî clears the plan's main worry); the
  assembly is closed term-mode via `hK_lhs.trans hK_rhs.symm`; `hK_rhs` is driven through the
  prefix-strip + braiding-collapse + keystone to the precise Œº-cancel; `hK_lhs` reduced to its
  isolated goal. All steps verified by cold build.
- **STOP-and-surface (plan's explicit condition):** the plan said "if `hK_rhs` won't close ‚Üí STOP
  and surface it; iter-013 corrective is a Mathlib-idiom consult on the `Localization.Monoidal`
  entry point." That condition has fired. **Key finding correcting the iter-011 hypothesis:**
  isolating the goal into the half-assemblies does **NOT** by itself let `Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc`
  fire ‚Äî the `CategoryStruct.comp` instance mismatch (localized `Localization.Monoidal` comp vs the
  `LocalizedMonoidal`-synonym/`X.Modules` comp) persists even on the small goal, and `Category.assoc`
  cannot bridge it. The remaining lever is the plan's `show`/`change` instance-pin of both Œº's to one
  literal localized-`comp` form before the cancel ‚Äî but a naive `simp only [Localization.Monoidal.Œº]`
  unfold times out, so the iter-013 consult should target: **what is the canonical instance-agnostic
  way to cancel `e.hom ‚â´ e.inv` across the `LocalizedMonoidal` synonym/base `comp` boundary?**
  (e.g. a `change` to `(L').obj`-level comp, or a dedicated `Localization.Monoidal` cancellation
  idiom). Once that single cancel is available, both halves close mechanically (hK_rhs: + Œº_natural_
  right + counit triangle; hK_lhs: + associator_naturality + associator_hom_app + the same cancel).

## Blueprint markers (for the review agent)
- `lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs` / `lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_rhs` (L1516/L1560 in the chapter) now have
  matching Lean decls `‚Ä¶_hK_lhs` / `‚Ä¶_hK_rhs` (both PARTIAL = `sorry` present ‚Üí statement-`\leanok`
  only, no proof-`\leanok`; sync_leanok will set this).
- `lem:tensorObjAssoc_eq_localizedAssociator`: the Lean own-body is sorry-free but transitively gated
  on the two half-lemmas ‚Äî sync_leanok should keep proof-`\leanok` OFF until the halves close.
- New def `assocCommonForm` realises the chapter's `K`; if the planner wants it blueprinted, it is
  the well-typed `K` (with the seg-1 unit prefix the schematic omits).

# Blueprint Writer Report: snap-mucancel

## Changes

- Revise `lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs` proof: deleted stale isolation note + iter-012 `% NOTE`; correct mechanism = `associator_naturality` pushes counit isos through `Œ±` ‚Üí `associator_hom_app` makes Œº's native ‚Üí Œº-pair cancels across localized/base comp boundary (peel leading Œº‚Åª¬π first) ‚Üí triangle + `Œº_natural_left` leave K. Repointed `\uses`‚Üí`def:assocCommonForm`.
- Revise `lem:tensorObjAssoc_hK_rhs` proof: replaced "syntactically same / Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc" with TWO stacked defeq mismatches (comp-instance + Œº object-arg) absorbed up-to-reducible-defeq, mandatory leading-Œº‚Åª¬π peel; residual closed by `Œº_natural_right` + counit triangle. Math content (segments, braiding, keystone) intact. Repointed `\uses`.
- Cited `analogies/snap-localized-comp-cancel.md` as mechanism authority in both proofs.

## Notes / Strategy
- leandag: 0 unknown_uses, `def:assocCommonForm` matched to Lean + non-isolated. No `\leanok` added by me. Project-bespoke ‚Äî no external SOURCE.

content:
# Lean ‚Üî Blueprint Check Report

## Slug
fbc012

## Iteration
012

## Files audited
- Lean: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean`
- Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`

Scope: the 4 new helper lemmas added this iter (L696‚Äì723) plus the still-open
`gammaPushforwardIso_comp` (L733, `sorry`@784) and its blueprint counterpart
`lem:gammaPushforwardIso_comp`. LSP diagnostics over L675‚Äì790 confirm the **only**
sorry/error in the region is L733; the four helpers emit no errors (only cosmetic
long-line warnings on comment lines L750‚Äì783).

## Per-declaration

### 4 new helpers (no `\lean{...}` block ‚Äî see "Unreferenced declarations")

#### `AlgebraicGeometry.moduleCat_eqToHom_concreteCategory_apply` (L696)
- **Lean target exists**: yes
- **Body genuine / non-fake**: yes ‚Äî `by subst h; rfl`. Substantive content is the
  `eqToHom`-as-`cast` identity; honest, compiles.
- **Blueprint block**: none (sub-`gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply` plumbing).
- **notes**: kernel-light replacement for a `change`/`rfl` collapse; faithful.

#### `AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIso_hom_concreteApply` (L706)
- **Lean target exists**: yes
- **Body genuine / non-fake**: yes ‚Äî `:= rfl`. A `ConcreteCategory.hom`-keyed
  restatement of the already-blueprinted `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply`. Compiles.
- **Blueprint block**: none (mechanical restatement of `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply`).

#### `AlgebraicGeometry.restrictScalars_map_concreteApply` (L713)
- **Lean target exists**: yes
- **Body genuine / non-fake**: yes ‚Äî `:= rfl`. "Restriction of scalars does not change
  the underlying function." Compiles.
- **Blueprint block**: none (generic `ModuleCat` plumbing).

#### `AlgebraicGeometry.restrictScalarsComp_inv_app_concreteApply` (L720)
- **Lean target exists**: yes
- **Body genuine / non-fake**: yes ‚Äî `:= rfl`. "`restrictScalarsComp.inv` is identity on
  carriers." Compiles.
- **Blueprint block**: none (generic `ModuleCat` plumbing).

**Verdict on the 4 helpers: all faithful and non-fake.** Each is a genuine single-layer
`rfl` / `subst;rfl`, verified to elaborate without error. None is a placeholder, none has
a suspicious body, none carries an excuse-comment. They are legitimate kernel-light glue
sitting *below* the level the blueprint formalizes (carrier-identity restatements of
`gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply` + generic `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` facts), so the absence
of `\lean{...}` blocks for them is correct, not a coverage gap.

### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIso_comp}` (chapter: `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_comp`, L794‚Äì837)
- **Lean target exists**: yes (L733).
- **Signature matches**: yes. The Lean statement (composite `(œÜ ‚â´ œÅ)` comparison =
  pushforwardComp/`Spec.map_comp`-cast domain reorg ‚â´ single-map comparisons ‚â´
  `restrictScalarsComp` codomain reorg) is a faithful encoding of the blueprint statement.
- **Proof follows sketch**: **no / N/A ‚Äî open `sorry`@784**, and the blueprint sketch
  diverges from the discovered Lean reality (see Blueprint adequacy below). This is a
  documented genuine WIP, *not* a fake/placeholder claiming completion: the body collapses
  the LHS via `gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply` and bottoms out in an honest `sorry` with a
  precise multi-line account of the blocker.
- **Markers consistent**: statement block has `\leanok` (correct ‚Äî declaration exists);
  proof block (L822) has **no** `\leanok` (correct ‚Äî `sorry` present). The
  `sync_leanok` markers are right here.

## Red flags

None of must-fix class.

- No fake/placeholder bodies: the four helpers are real `rfl`s; the lone `sorry`
  (`gammaPushforwardIso_comp`@784) is a documented open obligation, not a stand-in for a
  claim the blueprint marks complete.
- No excuse-comments masking wrong code. The long comment block L745‚Äì783 is an honest,
  precise blocker characterization (the value-`ModuleCat`/`X.Modules` object-junction
  diamond), not an "it's wrong but works" note.
- No axioms / `Classical.choice` on substantive claims introduced this iter.

## Stale docstrings (major)

### `gammaPushforwardNatIso_comp` docstring, `FlatBaseChange.lean:813`
The docstring asserts: *"Every constituent is identity-on-carriers (as is
`gammaPushforwardNatIso` itself), so the proof is pointwise reflexivity."* This is **stale
and mildly misleading**: the actual proof (L828‚Äì840) is **not** pointwise reflexivity ‚Äî it
`simp only [...]`-collapses the whiskering/associator pasting and then `exact
gammaPushforwardIso_comp œÜ œÅ N`, i.e. it *delegates* to the open, non-pointwise
`gammaPushforwardIso_comp`. The blueprint itself was already corrected on this point ‚Äî
`lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso_comp`'s proof (chapter L917) explicitly states *"That component
coherence is **not** a pointwise identity"* ‚Äî so the Lean docstring now contradicts both the
realized proof and the (updated) chapter. Recommend rewording the L813 docstring to "reduces
per-component to `gammaPushforwardIso_comp`" rather than "pointwise reflexivity."

(For contrast, the "pointwise `rfl`" phrasing in the `gammaPushforwardNatIso` docstring at
L795 is **accurate** ‚Äî that def really is `ext x; rfl` ‚Äî and the `gammaPushforwardIso_comp`
docstring/comment L725‚Äì783 is accurate and current. Only the L813 claim is stale.)

## Unreferenced declarations (informational)

- `moduleCat_eqToHom_concreteCategory_apply`, `gammaPushforwardIso_hom_concreteApply`,
  `restrictScalars_map_concreteApply`, `restrictScalarsComp_inv_app_concreteApply` ‚Äî all
  four are unreferenced by any `\lean{...}` block. **Acceptable as helpers**: they are
  carrier-level `rfl` plumbing strictly below the blueprint's granularity (the math content
  "the comparison is identity on carriers" is already captured by the blueprinted
  `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply` / `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_inv_apply`). No new blueprint
  blocks warranted; at most a one-line `% NOTE` could acknowledge them, but this is not
  required.

## Blueprint adequacy for this file

- **Coverage**: the substantive declarations of the file map to `\lean{...}` blocks
  (`gammaPushforwardIso_comp`, `gammaPushforwardNatIso_comp`, `gammaPushforwardIso_{hom,inv}_apply`,
  the locality / tilde / localization lemmas, etc.). The 4 new helpers are intentionally
  unblueprinted plumbing (acceptable). No substantive declaration is orphaned.
- **Proof-sketch depth for `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_comp`**: **under-specified / partly stale
  vs the discovered blocker (major).** The chapter proof (L822‚Äì837) treats the crux as
  *"Unwinding both sides to these underlying carriers, the asserted equality reduces to ‚Ä¶ the
  three-fold global-sections ring coherence `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp3_specMap_appTop`."*
  But the prover's realized route shows the genuine obstruction is **not** an application of
  that ring lemma ‚Äî once the LHS is collapsed by `gammaPushforwardIso_hom_apply` (a `rfl`)
  and the `Spec.map_comp` reindexing cast is seen to be carrier-identity (`Œì(cast) x = x`),
  the whole equation is a carrier identity, and the ring coherence is already *baked into the
  definition* of `gammaPushforwardIso` (via `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop`). The
  three-fold lemma `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp3_specMap_appTop` **does not appear anywhere in
  the proof attempt.** The actual wall is the value-`ModuleCat`/`X.Modules`
  **object-junction diamond**: carrier-`rfl` is cheap, but each wrapper changes the
  `ModuleCat` *object* type, so per-junction object-defeq is kernel-heavy ‚Äî a monolithic
  `rfl` is a verified cold-build `whnf` kernel bomb and the term-mode `Eq.trans` chain
  explodes (>1.6M heartbeats). The chapter's "unwinding to carriers" framing silently
  understates exactly this step. A prover working *from prose alone* would have been
  steered toward `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp3_specMap_appTop` and away from the real route
  (sheaf-level `.val.app (op ‚ä§)` via `pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app`, where the
  restrictScalars/globalSections junctions never form). The `\uses{‚Ä¶ 
  globalSectionsIso_hom_comp3_specMap_appTop}` annotation is likewise suspect for the
  realized route.
- **Hint precision**: precise ‚Äî `\lean{...}` targets all resolve to the right declarations
  with matching signatures.
- **Generality**: matches need.
- **Recommended chapter-side actions** (for a blueprint-writing subagent):
  1. In the `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_comp` proof (L822‚Äì837), add a `% NOTE:` (or a sentence)
     recording that the Lean obstruction is the carrier-cheap / object-defeq-heavy
     value-`ModuleCat` junction diamond, and that the realized route goes through the
     sheaf-level `.val.app (op ‚ä§)` (`pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app`) rather than a direct
     carrier unwinding. Re-examine whether `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp3_specMap_appTop` is
     genuinely invoked on the realized route or whether the ring coherence is subsumed by the
     definition of `gammaPushforwardIso`; adjust the `\uses` accordingly.
  2. (Lean-side, for the prover/plan, not the chapter) Reword the stale
     `gammaPushforwardNatIso_comp` docstring at `FlatBaseChange.lean:813` away from "pointwise
     reflexivity" to match the chapter's already-correct "not a pointwise identity; reduces
     per-component to `gammaPushforwardIso_comp`."

## Severity summary

- **must-fix-this-iter**: none. Signatures match the prose; the four helpers are genuine
  non-fake `rfl`s; the lone `sorry` is a documented WIP (not a placeholder on a
  blueprint-claims-complete declaration); `\leanok` markers are consistent with sorry status.
- **major**:
  - Blueprint proof sketch for `lem:gammaPushforwardIso_comp` is under-specified / partly
    stale vs the discovered blocker ‚Äî it points at `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp3_specMap_appTop`
    and treats carrier-unwinding as trivial, whereas the real obstruction is the value-
    `ModuleCat` object-junction defeq (kernel `whnf` bomb). Recommend the chapter `% NOTE`
    above (does not block the prover, who has already characterized the blocker in-file).
  - Stale Lean docstring at `FlatBaseChange.lean:813` ("the proof is pointwise reflexivity")
    contradicting both the realized delegating proof and the corrected chapter prose.
- **minor**:
  - 4 new helpers unreferenced by the blueprint ‚Äî acceptable as sub-granularity plumbing.

Overall verdict: the four new helpers are faithful, non-fake, kernel-light `rfl`s with no
red flags; the one open `sorry` (`gammaPushforwardIso_comp`) is an honestly-documented WIP,
but the chapter's proof sketch for it (and the stale L813 "pointwise reflexivity" docstring)
are now out of step with the prover's discovered value-`ModuleCat` object-junction blocker ‚Äî
two **major**, non-blocking, bidirectional findings to align prose with reality.

# Mathlib Analogist: snap-localized-comp-cancel
**Mode:** api-alignment | **Iter:** 013

## Answer (VALIDATED on the real `hK_rhs` goal, cold LSP ‚Äî closes the cancel, 0 errors)
The cancel idiom is **`erw` (up-to-defeq), NOT `show`/`change`, NOT a new lemma**:
```lean
erw [Category.assoc]                        -- cross the boundary ONCE: pull leading Œº.inv out (clean)
refine congrArg (fun t => _ ‚â´ t) ?_         -- peel the common leading Œº.inv  (SHIELD ‚Äî mandatory)
erw [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]  -- reassoc across boundary + cancel Œº.hom ‚â´ Œº.inv
```
Leaves EXACTLY the directive's residual: `(A‚ô≠‚óÅŒ∑^#) ‚â´ (c_A‚äóc_{B‚äóC}) ‚â´ A‚óÅ(Œº_{B,C}.inv ‚â´ (c_B‚äóc_C))
= (A‚ô≠‚óÅŒº_{B,C}.inv) ‚â´ (c_A‚äó(c_B‚äóc_C))` ‚Üí close with `Œº_natural_right` + counit triangle `L'Œ∑ ‚â´ c = ùüô`.

## Why (root cause, verified)
- `LocalizedMonoidal L W Œµ := D` with `Category := inferInstanceAs (Category D)`
  (`‚Ä¶/Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean:86,94`). Project's `D` = sheafification codomain is defeq-but-not-
  syntactic to `X.Modules`. So localized comp = `@CategoryStruct.comp (LocalizedMonoidal‚Ä¶) (inferInstanceAs‚Ä¶)`
  vs boundary comp = `@CategoryStruct.comp X.Modules instCategory`: **distinct instance terms, defeq only**.
- Mathlib NEVER mixes them ‚Äî all its coherences (`pentagon :329`, `triangle :412`, `associator_naturality
  :286`) run INSIDE `LocalizedMonoidal` (one instance) and cancel Œº-pairs with plain `simp`/`Iso.*_assoc`.
  The project's boundary is self-inflicted: the keystone `sheafification_whiskerLeft_unit_eq_mu'` (`:1800`,
  localized comps) is `erw`'d into an `X.Modules`-comp context (`tensorObjAssoc` + `sheafification.map` +
  the `Œº.inv` from `tensorObjLocalizedIso :1462`).
- TWO stacked mismatches at the pair: comp-instance + Œº object-arg (`B.tensorObj C` vs
  `sheafification.obj(B‚ô≠‚äóC‚ô≠)`). `erw` absorbs BOTH up to reducible defeq.

## Verified-DEAD (so blueprint must not prescribe them)
`rw [Category.assoc]` (pattern not found ‚Äî instances differ), `simp only [Category.assoc]` (no progress),
`dsimp only []` (no progress), `conv_lhs => rw [Category.assoc]` (no progress), targeted `show ‚Ä¶inv ‚â´ _`
(shape-fails), `rw [show obj(B.tensorObj C)=obj(sheafification.obj‚Ä¶) from rfl]` (motive-not-type-correct),
`erw [Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]`/`erw[Category.assoc,Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]` w/o the peel (adjacency not reached).

## HAZARD (must surface in the recipe)
An **unshielded** 2nd `erw [Category.assoc]` whnf-unfolds the leading `Œº.inv` ‚Üí `Functor.curry.mapIso
(Localization.fac ‚Ä¶)` (the isDefEq blow-up the directive feared from `simp [Monoidal.Œº]`). The `congrArg`
leading-`Œº.inv` peel (the proof's own idiom, `:1919-1920`) is **mandatory** to prevent it.

## Verdicts
- **cancel idiom**: PROCEED with the `erw`+peel recipe (validated). Prover's `show`/`change` candidate
  REFUTED (full `show` impractical; targeted `show` shape-fails; `change` forces a giant term + Œº-whnf risk).
  No dedicated `Localization.Monoidal` cancel lemma needed.
- **`hK_lhs`**: same cancel applies after `associator_naturality`+`associator_hom_app` expose the Œº-pairs.
- **strategic (flagged)**: DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY now; Mathlib-aligned fix is option (c) ‚Äî keep the bridge in
  ONE category (`modulesLocalizedMonoidal X`) so plain `simp` closes all 5 coherences. Revisit if `erw` cost
  compounds. Not a this-iter must-fix.

## Persistent file
- `analogies/snap-localized-comp-cancel.md` written.

Overall verdict: cancel SOLVED ‚Äî `erw`+`congrArg`-peel crosses the defeq comp boundary and the Œº object-arg
defeq in one shot; the `show`/`change` candidate is refuted and no new lemma is required.
